variable.description: Environment variable assignment. No spaces around =. Loaded into process environment.

comment.description: Lines starting with # are comments and are ignored.

quoted_value.description: Double-quoted values can contain spaces and some escape sequences. Single quotes are treated literally.

multiline.description: Multi-line values use double quotes with \n for newlines, or actual newlines within quotes.

interpolation.description: Variable expansion references other variables. Supported by dotenv-expand and some loaders.

export.description: The export prefix makes the variable available to child processes. Optional in .env files.

DATABASE_URL.description: Standard database connection URL format. Supports postgres://, mysql://, mongodb://.

NODE_ENV.description: Node.js environment variable. Controls debug output, optimizations, and dependency behavior.

PORT.description: The port number the application listens on. Common convention across frameworks.

env_file.description: Files are loaded in order: .env (shared), .env.local (local overrides), .env.{NODE_ENV} (environment-specific).

gitignore.description: .env files with secrets should NEVER be committed to git. Add to .gitignore and use .env.example as a template.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
